2026-04-10T18:18:29.6365748Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6365993Z   ---
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6366243Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6366556Z   ### 📌 Title
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6367011Z   **CI: Indirect batches should also cover PackageReference dependencies**
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6367845Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6368299Z   ---
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6368544Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6368851Z   ### 🔖 Metadata
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6369130Z   | Field | Value |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6369450Z   |---|---|
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6369727Z   | **State** | `open` |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6370066Z   | **Author** | `JoshLove-msft` |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6370495Z   | **Author Association** | `MEMBER` |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6370888Z   | **Created** | 2026-04-10T18:15:20Z |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6371269Z   | **Labels** | `Batch` |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6371599Z   | **Comments** | 0 |
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6371876Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6372106Z   ---
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6372340Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6372664Z   ### 📝 Body
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6372916Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6373132Z   **## Summary**
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6373405Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6375266Z   When a breaking change (e.g., type forwarding) is introduced in a core package like `Azure.Core`, the indirect CI batches only rebuild and test projects that use **ProjectReference** to that package. Projects that depend on affected types via **PackageReference** (e.g., `Azure.Identity`) are not included in the indirect batch and can silently break.
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6377215Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6377472Z   **## Example**
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6377722Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6380259Z   PR [#58049](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58049) hit CS0433 errors in `Azure.Analytics.Purview.DataMap` and `Azure.Analytics.OnlineExperimentation` test projects because `DefaultAzureCredential` now exists in both `Azure.Core` (v1.53.0) and `Azure.Identity`. These test projects reference `Azure.Identity` via `PackageReference`, so they were not caught by the indirect CI batch when the Core changes landed.
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6382584Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6382860Z   **## Recommendation**
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6383177Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6384406Z   Consider expanding indirect CI batches to also include projects that have a **PackageReference** to packages affected by the change, or add a periodic full-repo validation build that catches these cross-package type conflicts.
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6385914Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6386156Z   ---
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6386397Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6386793Z   ### 💬 Comments
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6387089Z   *(No comments yet)*
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6387363Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6387619Z   ---
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6387851Z   
2026-04-10T18:18:29.6389600Z   **Summary:** This is an open issue filed by a repo `MEMBER` requesting that the CI indirect batch system be enhanced to also trigger builds for packages that depend on changed packages via `PackageReference` (not just `ProjectReference`), to prevent silent breakage from type conflicts.
